title: Ulex vaillantii
intro: Ulex vaillantii, also known as Vaillant's gorse, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, Vaillant's gorse has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Ulex vaillantii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vaillant's gorse include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Ulex vaillantii is a species of spiny shrub in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is classified as Ulex vaillantii (Bull) Nyman, which reflects its former classification by Bulliard and later reclassification by Nyman. The Ulex vaillantii species is a member of the subgenus Ulex, which consists of a variety of spiny shrubs. The classification of Ulex vaillantii is also based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other species in the Fabaceae family.
